reason: Both answers correctly identify density (not length) as the real axis, both affirm depth drives engineering-brand authority, and both push back on an arbitrary 1,200-word target while giving the same actionable prescription: cut throat-clearing/backstory, keep hard-won specifics, improve skimmability, and let final length fall out of the edit. Differences are minor: A frames the verdict as "mostly the author" with a scroll-depth/shares heuristic; B frames it as "reviewer closer on instinct" with a concrete cut estimate (20-30%, ~2,500-2,800 words). B's estimate is slightly more specific, but A's framing better matches the key insight that the reviewer is reacting to flab rather than length itself. These roughly offset, and neither adds a genuinely new decision-relevant fact the other lacks.</reason>
